title: PayPal vs Crypto for Building a Shopify Alternative With Crypto Payments — An Honest Comparison
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip do not work in your country. That is a platform problem, not a you problem. Here is what actually works.

content.text: Having a great digital product to sell is only half the battle, especially when you're a creator in a country like Nigeria or Ghana, where payment platforms like PayPal and Stripe are not available, leaving you wondering how to collect payments from customers, this is a frustrating reality that many digital creators face, and it's a problem that Unchained Commerce is dedicated to solving, by providing a platform that allows creators to sell their products without being held back by geographic restrictions or banking limitations
What You Are Actually Comparing

When comparing PayPal to crypto for building a Shopify alternative with crypto payments, it's essential to understand the underlying mechanics of each system, PayPal and other traditional platforms rely on complex compliance decisions and fraud risk tiers, which often result in creators from certain countries being excluded, whereas crypto-native infrastructure operates without these geographic restrictions, providing a more inclusive and accessible payment solution
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Traditional payment platforms like PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip often break down for creators in restricted countries due to their stringent KYC requirements and banking infrastructure limitations, which can lead to account suspensions or outright rejection, this can be devastating for creators who rely on these platforms to sell their digital products, and it's a problem that crypto-native infrastructure can help solve
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

The buyer experience in a crypto store is simpler than most creators expect when they first hear about it. The buyer selects a coin — USDT, BTC, ETH, SOL, TRX — and receives a QR code and wallet address generated specifically for their order. They send the payment from their wallet. The transaction confirms on chain, typically within minutes for USDT on Tron or SOL. The store detects the confirmation automatically through the NOWPayments webhook and delivers the digital product to the buyer's email. The buyer does not need an account on your store. They do not need to provide identity verification. They need a crypto wallet and the product you made. The experience from purchase to delivery takes under ten minutes for most transactions and requires no manual step from the creator at any point.
The reality is that traditional payment platforms like PayPal and Stripe are not designed to serve creators in restricted countries, and it's time to look for alternative solutions
The Real Cost Difference

While traditional payment platforms may seem like a convenient option, their costs can add up quickly, especially when you factor in transaction fees, currency conversion rates, and other charges, crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, often provides a more cost-effective solution, with lower transaction fees and no need for intermediaries, which can help creators keep more of their hard-earned money
Which One to Use and Why

Keeping the store working requires less ongoing attention than creators expect because automation handles the operational layer entirely. Orders log automatically to the Admin Dashboard every time a payment confirms. Delivery fires automatically every time an order logs. Receipts generate and attach automatically every time delivery fires. The creator has full visibility into every transaction without doing any manual tracking. The weekly routine for a running store is: check the Admin Dashboard to confirm deliveries are completing, check your NOWPayments account to confirm configured coins are active, review which coins are generating volume. Everything else runs without you. That is the operational picture for a creator who solved the platform restriction problem at the infrastructure level rather than trying to work around it.
What Creators in Restricted Countries Are Asking

If you're a creator in a restricted country, you may be wondering how to sell digital products without PayPal, or how to get paid online if Stripe is not available in your country, the good news is that there are alternatives available, such as crypto-native infrastructure, which can provide a more inclusive and accessible payment solution, and with the help of platforms like Unchained Commerce, you can also automate delivery, payment verification, and other essential tasks, making it easier to sell your digital products and get paid online
Crypto-native infrastructure is not just a payment solution, it's a key to unlocking platform independence and reaching customers all over the world
Related Topics Worth Reading

For creators who are looking for a Gumroad alternative or a way to sell online without a Stripe account, crypto-native infrastructure can provide a solution, by providing a more inclusive and accessible payment option, creators can sell their digital products without being held back by geographic restrictions or banking limitations, and with the help of platforms like Unchained Commerce, they can also get access to a range of tools and features that make it easy to sell digital products and get paid online
Your Launch Checklist — No Platform Permission Required

Before launching your digital product store, make sure you have a solid foundation in place, this includes setting up a Core Shop Engine to handle automated delivery and payment verification, integrating Web3 Wallet Connect to provide a seamless payment experience for your customers, and using an Admin Dashboard to track transactions and orders, with these tools in place, you'll be well on your way to creating a successful digital product store that can reach customers all over the world

Success as a digital creator means being able to sell your products and get paid online, without being held back by geographic restrictions or banking limitations, and with the right tools and infrastructure in place, you can achieve that
